title: Nagpur NEET aspirant gets Abu Dhabi as exam centre
content: A Nagpur NEET aspirant received a shocking hall ticket assigning his exam centre to Abu Dhabi, over 2,500 km away, despite not possessing a passport. The National Testing Agency (NTA) assured rectification, but with the exam imminent, the family faced immense stress arranging travel. This incident follows widespread reports of exam centre changes, even for students who opted to retain their original locations, adding to the anxiety post-paper leak.

title: 'Do you take Eknath seriously?' Sanjay Raut on Shinde's 'yeh toh trailer hai' remark
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Sanjay Raut has strongly criticized Maharashtra Deputy CM Eknath Shinde, labeling him a "dishonest leader" who betrayed the party. Raut also drew parallels with West Bengal's Suvendu Adhikari, suggesting both leaders make provocative statements while in power. He warned that public backlash awaits them once their power diminishes, amidst speculation of MPs switching parties.

title: 'Go to auction': Ex-cricketer urges KL Rahul to leave DC as Pant set to return
content: Rishabh Pant is reportedly set for a homecoming to Delhi Capitals, potentially involving a trade with Kuldeep Yadav moving to Lucknow Super Giants. Amidst this, former India cricketer Dodda Ganesh has advised KL Rahul to seek an auction if Pant returns. Rahul has excelled for Delhi Capitals, while Pant's stint with Lucknow was less successful, prompting Ganesh's suggestion for Rahul to consider his options.

title: Google AI CEO shares 'thanks note' as Nobel winner AI researcher leaves DeepMind
content: Google DeepMind is experiencing another significant departure as Vice President John Jumper, a Nobel laureate for his work on AI protein structure prediction with AlphaFold, is heading to Anthropic. This follows closely on the heels of Noam Shazeer, a key AI researcher and Gemini co-lead, announcing his move to OpenAI. Both researchers were instrumental in groundbreaking AI advancements at Google.

title: Punjabi teen arrested for killing parents and grandmother in US
content: A 19-year-old of Punjabi origin, Gaurav Chopra, has been arrested in Texas for allegedly killing his parents and grandmother. The incident occurred just months after the family visited their native village in India.  His brother narrowly escaped the attack, reporting Gaurav fled the scene. Investigators are seeking a motive for the shocking crime.

title: 'Thought it was terrorist explosion': London train crash survivors 'had broken legs', recalls horror
content: A tragic train collision near Bedford claimed the life of a driver and left over 80 injured, with survivors recounting scenes of panic and devastation. Passengers described being violently thrown, witnessing smoke, and hearing screams as emergency services rushed to the site. The incident involved two East Midlands Railway services heading towards London St Pancras, leading to a complete suspension of services.

title: 'We don't get to choose..': What Google CEO Sundar Pichai advices to students
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai, addressing Stanford's Class of 2026, urged graduates to embrace optimism and tackle challenging endeavors. He shared personal anecdotes, emphasizing that meaningful work stems from genuine passion rather than external expectations. Pichai's core message highlighted the importance of perseverance and continuous forward movement, regardless of life's inevitable uncertainties.

title: Bolivia crisis explained: State of emergency declared after 50 days of protests
content: Bolivia's President Rodrigo Paz has declared a state of emergency after nearly 50 days of crippling protests over economic hardship.  The move allows the military to clear widespread road blockades that have led to severe shortages of essential goods.  Demonstrations, fueled by anger over rising costs and subsidy cuts, are demanding the president's resignation, despite recent government concessions.

title: Sam Altman is hiring Trump Admin's AI policy adviser Dean Ball
content: OpenAI has appointed former Trump administration AI policy advisor Dean Ball to lead its new Strategic Futures unit. Ball will focus on frontier AI policy and governance, aiming to shape the nascent field. This move highlights OpenAI's commitment to bolstering its policy expertise as it navigates global regulatory discussions and its mission to ensure AI benefits all of humanity.

title: 'Kuch log...': Sanjay Raut counters Shinde's 'sher akela aata hai' remark with loyal dog post
content: Political tensions escalate between Shiv Sena factions as Sanjay Raut fires back at Eknath Shinde's 'lion' jibe with a post calling some people disloyal dogs. This follows speculation of six Uddhav Thackeray camp MPs potentially defecting to Shinde's side. Raut announced action, including disqualification efforts, against the absent MPs, while Thackeray emotionally appealed to workers, vowing not to hand the party to 'thieves'.

title: Fix it, flip it — how to turn a run-down home into a money-maker
content: House flipping, a popular investment strategy, involves buying distressed properties, renovating them, and selling for a profit. Success hinges on finding undervalued homes, meticulously managing renovation budgets to avoid unexpected costs like plumbing or foundation issues, and timing the market for a quick sale. While offering substantial returns and the satisfaction of transformation, this venture carries inherent risks due to significant upfront investment and fluctuating market conditions.

title: OSSSC RI, ARI exam postponed due to NEET UG: Check new date & fresh admit card details
content: The Odisha Subordinate Staff Selection Commission (OSSSC) has rescheduled its Combined Recruitment Examination (CRE)-2025, originally set for June 20, 2026, to June 28, 2026. This postponement, affecting posts like Revenue Inspector and ICDS Supervisor, is due to unavoidable circumstances related to the NEET (UG) 2026 examination. Candidates will receive new admit cards by June 24, 2026, with all other recruitment conditions remaining unchanged

title: 77-year-old dies as IIT graduate pushes him after parking spat in Noida
content: A heated parking dispute between a 77-year-old retired bank employee and his 30-year-old IIT engineer neighbour in Noida tragically ended with the elder man's death. The elderly victim was allegedly pushed to the ground during the argument, sustaining injuries. He passed away hours later in the hospital, prompting police to register a case of culpable homicide not amounting to murder against the neighbour.

title: As Moscow burns, spotlight once again on loitering munition, India keeps pace with global trend
content: Unmanned drones have transformed into strategic weapons, capable of deep strikes and economic disruption. Ukraine's recent large-scale drone assaults on Russian oil refineries highlight this shift, undermining war economies and demonstrating vulnerability. Iran's long-range Shahed drones project power across the Middle East, challenging adversaries through sheer volume. India is also integrating indigenous kamikaze drones into its doctrine for precision strikes, underscoring the global evolution of drones as potent strategic assets.

title: How captaincy has fueled Shubman Gill's rise as India's new run machine
content: Shubman Gill's captaincy has coincided with a remarkable surge in his batting prowess across formats. Since taking charge, he's transformed into a dependable scorer, notching centuries at an impressive rate. This leadership role appears to have unlocked a new level, with his Test average nearly doubling and his run-scoring significantly boosted, raising questions about the impact of the armband on his game.

title: Crude comeback: 20 million barrels leave Iran port after peace breakthrough
content: Iran's oil exports have surged following a peace deal with Washington, with 11 tankers carrying 20 million barrels departing the Gulf of Oman.  This increase coincides with new maritime regulations for the Strait of Hormuz.  However, the delay of permanent peace talks due to regional clashes casts a shadow on the long-term outlook, with lighter traffic observed on Friday.

title: 'Just being cute, funny': Donald Trump clarifies 'I'm the boss' remark at G7 summit
content: US President Donald Trump has clarified his "I'm the boss" remark at the G7 summit, stating it was a lighthearted joke intended to be funny, not an assertion of authority.  He explained the comment arose from the unusual seating arrangement at the summit.  Trump's clarification comes amid significant diplomatic developments, including a US-Iran agreement.

title: INS Dunagiri, Sanshodhak, Agray: Navy to commission 3 indigenous warships and survey vessel
content: India's maritime strength is set to surge as PM Modi commissions three new indigenous warships in Kolkata on June 21. INS Dunagiri, a stealth frigate, INS Sanshodhak, a survey vessel, and INS Agray, an anti-submarine warfare craft, will bolster the Indian Navy's combat, surveillance, and deep-sea capabilities. These platforms, boasting over 75% indigenous content, signify a major leap in India's self-reliance in defense manufacturing.

title: Citizens can seek damages if right to walk is violated: SC
content: The Supreme Court has declared the right to walk a fundamental right, integral to life and free movement.  The apex court urged the government to enact a law ensuring well-demarcated footpaths on all roads, prioritizing pedestrian safety over motorized vehicles. This landmark ruling empowers citizens to seek legal recourse for violations and mandates civic bodies to maintain pedestrian infrastructure, transforming urban and rural living.

title: The great reverse migration: Why India's professionals are leaving Metro cities
content: A fascinating trend is emerging as Indian professionals increasingly desert sprawling metropolises in favor of tier-2 and tier-3 cities, searching for a richer quality of life. The rising costs of living, extensive travel for work, and the stress of city life are significant forces behind this migration.

title: 2 Khalistani operatives involved in targeting rail corridor in Punjab deported from Malaysia
content: Two key operatives of the banned Khalistan Zindabad Force (KZF), Gurwinder Singh and Manjeet Singh, have been deported from Malaysia following a joint intelligence and police operation. Apprehended in Delhi, they are accused of being crucial links in a terror module targeting railway infrastructure in Punjab. Investigations revealed their role in channeling funds from Malaysia to support planned attacks aimed at disrupting peace in the state.

title: Afghanistan claims strikes on militant hideouts inside Pakistan
content: Afghanistan's Taliban government claims its forces conducted airstrikes on militant hideouts in Pakistan's Balochistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa provinces. Islamabad vehemently denies this, stating only a drone entered its airspace and was intercepted. This latest exchange escalates tensions, with both nations blaming each other for cross-border militancy and recent deadly incidents, despite Chinese mediation efforts.

title: 'Major win for India': Vivek Aggarwal elected as FATF vice president
content: India has achieved a significant leadership role in the Financial Action Task Force (FATF) with Vivek Aggarwal elected as its Vice President. This appointment underscores India's commitment to combating money laundering and terrorist financing globally. Aggarwal's extensive experience, including leading India's FATF delegation and heading the Financial Intelligence Unit, will bolster the organization's efforts to secure financial integrity and dismantle illicit financial systems.

title: Selfie esteem: Meloni calls out Trump’s tall tale
content: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has boldly rejected Donald Trump's assertion that she pleaded for a selfie during a G7 summit, deeming his claims as completely 'fabricated.' She emphasized that neither herself nor Italy resorts to begging. This moment underlines Trump's unique diplomatic style, where he often embellishes relationships with global leaders like Emmanuel Macron and Mohammed bin Salman, spinning tales of admiration and desperation from foreign officials.

title: Bihar court grants 'Khan Sir' interim relief, upholds 'no coercive action' order
content: A Bihar court granted interim relief to educator Faisal Khan, known as "Khan Sir," in an anticipatory bail plea.  No coercive action will be taken against him until the next hearing. This follows a complaint filed by Roshan Anand of Gyan Bindu GS Academy regarding the death of his brother and an alleged assault. The development occurs after Anand's own bail in a separate coaching centre attack case.

title: Watch: Qatar's $400 mn ‘world’s most luxurious plane’ joins Trump's Air Force One fleet
content: President Trump unveiled a $400 million Boeing 747 gifted by Qatar as a temporary Air Force One, sparking criticism over its value and potential impact on defense projects.  The luxurious jet, featuring a striking new livery, will serve until new presidential aircraft arrive in 2027-2028.  Trump defended the gift, citing the need to match foreign leaders' modern fleets and the retirement of an aging veteran aircraft.

title: India's 2027 World Cup plans may hinge on Kuldeep rediscovering his rhythm
content: Kuldeep Yadav's place may not be under immediate threat, but his form has become a talking point. As India begin building towards the 2027 ODI World Cup, the team management is backing the left-arm wrist spinner to rediscover his wicket-taking touch and lead the middle overs once again.

title: Falta candidate Jahangir Khan's wife arrested in bid to free TMC leader from police custody
content: West Bengal Police have apprehended Sarina Bibi, wife of former TMC leader Jahangir Khan, for allegedly masterminding an attack on Falta police station. The incident aimed to free Khan from custody. This arrest is part of a wider crackdown on Khan's alleged criminal network, following his own apprehension near the India-Nepal border. Investigations into the attack are ongoing, with further arrests anticipated.

title: A tale of two cross-votings: How power politics played out in Karnataka, Jharkhand
content: Cross-voting significantly impacted Rajya Sabha elections in Jharkhandand Legislative Council elections in Karnataka, with both the INDIA bloc and NDA facing losses. While parties lauded rival cross-voters as exercising conscience, they condemned their own MLAs for betraying the party. This highlights a persistent double standard, where power dynamics, not ideology, drive these electoral outcomes despite past reforms aimed at curbing corruption.

title: ‘Balan’ movie review: A supremely crafted film in which everyone delivers their best
content: The Malayalam film ‘Balan’ is elevated by a strong technical team and fine performances. Director Chidambaram yet again proves his mastery over narrating a unique story

title: Monsoon tracker LIVE | Heavy rain expected in 10 districts of Tamil Nadu
content: The latest weather bulletin from the Regional Meteorological Centre on Saturday, June 20, said that a significant shift in weather patterns was expected from Sunday, June 21, paving the way for the onset of the Southwest Monsoon.

title: BJP busy shopping from other parties but common person can't afford necessities: Congress
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge alleged that households are perishing under the weight of the Modi government's "mismanagement of the economy"

title: Andhra Pradesh aims to become global tourism destination, says Minister Kandula Durgesh
content: Highlighting the achievements of the coalition government in the last two years, he says tourism has been granted industry status and introduced Tourism Policy 2024-29, attracting investments worth nearly ₹20,000 crore

title: Gatherings banned around NEET exam centres in Cyberabad on June 21; nearby photocopy shops to remain shut
content: In a notification issued on Friday (June 19), Cyberabad Police Commissioner M. Ramesh invoked Section 163 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS) and prohibited the assembly of five or more persons within a 200-metre radius of all NEET examination centres.

title: Gatherings barred, curbs imposed around 16 centres in Dehradun for NEET re-exam
content: To eliminate distractions for students, the use of loudspeakers, political sloganeering, provocative speeches and the distribution of misleading literature have also been banned around the perimeters

title: Kerala Police rope in religious leaders for anti-drug drive ‘Operation Toofan’
content: Home Minister Chennithala declares Samastha Kerala Jem-iyyathul Ulama State president Syed Mohammed Jifri Muthukoya Thangal and Sunni leader Kanthapuram A.P. Aboobacker Musliar as ‘Toofan warriors’

title: Vivek Aggarwal appointed Financial Action Task Force vice-president
content: Currently serving as Secretary in Culture Ministry, Aggarwal’s selection is a significant development for India’s role in global financial governance and efforts to strengthen international cooperation on anti-money laundering, counter-terrorist financing and illicit financial flows

title: NEET UG re-exam: Nagpur student gets Abu Dhabi centre; NTA to correct ‘allotment error’
content: The NTA has acknowledged the lapse on its part and stated it was a technical glitch and has assured that a revised admit card with Nagpur as centre of choice for the aggrieved student

title: M.P. to form dedicated task force to counter organised forest crime: CM Yadav
content: The Chief Minister also approved formation of a command and control centre for the protection of State’s forests and wildlife

title: Stop gambling with children's future: Rahul slams government over NEET aspirant exam centre mix-up
content: The family a Nagpur NEET aspirant claimed that he was allocated a school in Abu Dhabi as his exam centre for the re-test on June 21

title: President, PM to walk side by side in Odisha’s tribal hinterland
content: With India’s two tallest leaders set to share the stage, Odisha is in line for a host of development projects

title: Messi, Ronaldo, Neymar everywhere: Why Kerala’s Kochi is dressed for FIFA World Cup
content: Argentina’s three shades of blue and Brazil’s canary yellow unsurprisingly dominate the jersey market, followed by those of Portugal, Germany, Spain, England, Belgium, and others

title: Abhishek Banerjee says armed individuals at airport posed security threat; Trinamool claims ‘murder bid’
content: The party’s general secretary was one of the first leaders to come under physical attack after they lost the elections on May 4

title: Maradu flats case in Kerala: Ex-CPI(M) panchayat president, builders, architect and officials charge-sheeted
content: Former Maradu panchayat president and CPI(M) leader K. A. Devassy, former Maradu panchayat secretary M. Muhammad Ashraf, former junior superintendent P. E. Joseph and former upper division clerk R. Jayaram Naik were arraigned as the accused in all three cases booked by Crime Branch

title: Karnataka open to talks with Tamil Nadu on Mekedatu project, says Irrigation Minister Ramalinga Reddy
content: Maintaining that the project was being pursued within the legal framework laid down by courts and statutory authorities, the Minister said Karnataka would utilise any opportunity for dialogue to resolve the issue amicably

title: Kerala Budget: New Budget has a long wish list, but not enough money allocated to make it a reality, says Thomas Isaac
content: The CPI(M) leader says Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan who had talked at length about resource mobilisation in his podcasts do not have any such measures spelled out in the Budget

title: Schools in Cyberabad may go online one-day-a-week to tackle traffic chaos during monsoon
content: School pick-ups, drop-offs and buses are once again choking key corridors with Cyberabad police exploring weekly-once online classes during the monsoon.

title: Pakistani national who inadvertently crossed LoC repatriated
content: A Pakistani national, Asad Khan, a resident of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, was apprehended in Simari village, Kupwara, on June 12 after crossing the Line of Control

title: Former Odisha CM lashes out at current BJP govt for disruptions in disbursement of pensions
content: “It is important to recall that for the past 24 years, my government ensured that pensions were distributed regularly on the 15th of every month, observed as ‘Jan Seva Diwas,” says Naveen Patnaik

title: Modi ‘tough cookie’, ‘great leader’ who has been in power for over 12 years, says Trump
content: Donald Trump referred to China’s President Xi Jinping and Narendra Modi as the two greatest leaders he likes the most in terms of power and leverage, and the ability to execute on that

title: What is the Pawanraje Nimbalkar murder case? | Explained
content: The trial for the Pawanraje Nimbalkar murder case has been in progress at the Sessions Court in Mumbai since its commencement in 2011

title: Palaniswami objects to amended version of resolution against Mekedatu
content: In a post on social media, Mr. Palaniswami explained that the amendment proposed by Leader of Opposition Udhayanidhi Stalin was inserted into the resolution in violation of the rules of the House

title: NEET UG re-exam: Aspirants in Kerala hope for a glitch-free session on June 21
content: About 1.1 lakh students in Kerala registered for the original NEET UG exam held on May 3, as per official estimates

title: No provision for Kerala to withdraw from PM SHRI deal: Chief Minister V. D. Satheesan
content: Chief Minister says only Union government had the right to withdraw from the PM SHRI agreement after giving a notice of one month. There was no provision in the agreement to keep the scheme implementation in abeyance either

title: Madras High Court directs T.N. govt. to consider demand to increase university faculty retirement age to 65 years
content: The TNFUFA’s representation was in the wake of several State-run universities struggling with acute shortage of faculty and in some cases, falling short of the minimum number of faculty members mandated by the UGC

title: Telangana doctors oppose move to raise retirement age of medical college faculty from 65 to 70 years
content: The Telangana Junior Doctors Association (T-JUDA), Telangana Senior Residents Doctors Association (T-SRDA) and Healthcare Reforms Doctors Association (HRDA) issued separate statements on Saturday, June 20 urging the State government to retain the existing retirement age.

title: NEET-UG re-exam: Nationwide mock drill under way; security tightened
content: The nationwide mock drill is aimed at testing preparedness at examination centres and streamlining coordination among various agencies involved in the process

title: Despite grave concerns raised in the White Paper, Kerala Budget silent on bringing down committed expenditure
content: Committed expenditure is projected to exceed ₹1.22 lakh crore in 2026-27, with salaries, pensions and interest payments consuming nearly three-fourths of Kerala’s revenues

title: Chennai gets respite from searing heat as cloud cover, sea breeze cool city
content: The mercury level in the city was around 32 degree Celsius at 10 a.m. on Saturday, much lower than its average temperature of 37 degree Celsius recorded in June

title: FTCCI to organise three-day IITEX 2026 from June 26 in Hyderabad
content: The exhibition is expected to host over 100 exhibitors and attract more than 15,000 business decision-makers and visitors from different industrial sectors, FTCCI said

title: Cyberabad begins re-verification of 1.37 lakh ‘Nenu Saitham’ CCTV cameras
content: The police commissioner official further instructed officers to ensure that all cameras under the initiative are geo-tagged and that their records are regularly updated

title: Government clears 55 projects worth ₹7,506 crore, to create over 28,000 jobs in Karnataka
content: Of these, 41 projects would be located outside Bengaluru Urban and Bengaluru Rural districts, as part of the State’s Beyond Bengaluru initiative

title: Tamil Nadu government transfers 10 IAS officers
content: V.P. Jeyaseelan, Joint Commissioner (Health), Greater Chennai Corporation, has been transferred and posted as Director of Tourism, replacing M.S. Shanmugam, who is retiring on June 30

title: Doctor suspended, three others face action after pregnant woman’s death in Bhadradri Kothagudem
content: Departmental action has been initiated against ASHA workers for allegedly failing to identify severe anaemia in a pregnant woman and for not notifying the Medical Officer or ensuring timely referral to a higher healthcare facility

title: 3-language policy not viable, say CBSE schools in Nagaland
content: Principals from 19 schools urge Education Minister to grant the State a flexible framework as it is home to over 17 major recognised tribes each with their own distinct language and oral tradition

title: Cabinet meeting to be held in Coastal Karnataka, says Chief Minister
content: Mr. Shivakumar said the employment potential of the coastal region should be expanded further and called for greater focus on economic growth through tourism.

title: Dongria Kondh of Niyamgiri: Where forests, food and faith shape daily life
content: An intimate glimpse into the lives of the Dongria Kondh, one of India’s Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups, in Odisha’s Niyamgiri hills

title: Jyotipriya Mallick: The Mamata Banerjee Loyalist Who Has Quit Trinamool
content: Jyotipriya Mallick is a founding-era worker of the Trinamool Congress and a three-time minister in the erstwhile Mamata Banerjee government.

title: Arrested Iranian Singer Parastoo Ahmadi To Get 74 'Lashes' For Performing Without Hijab
content: A criminal court in Iran's Qom province ordered 74 stokes for the group, along with a two-year ban on leaving the country and a two-year restriction on participating in artistic activities.

title: Who Is Vivek Aggarwal, 1st Indian To Serve As Global Terror Watchdog FATF's Veep
content: Currently serving as secretary, Ministry of Culture, Vivek Aggarwal is recognised for his expertise in fiscal management, public administration and policy implementation.

title: US-Iran War News Live Updates: Trump's Envoy, Iran's Minister Head To Switzerland For Talks
content: US-Iran Peace Talks Live Updates: JD Vance had on Thursday cancelled his trip to Switzerland amid rising tension in Lebanon between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah.

title: Shiv Sena (UBT) Crisis Highlights: 6 Team Uddhav Rebels Get 'Y-Plus' Security Amid Threats
content: Uddhav Thackeray Shiv Sena Crisis Live: Shiv Sena (Uddhav camp) MP Sanjay Raut alleged that Rs 15 crore was being paid individually to "buy MPs".

title: The Modi Government Wants To Reverse India's Brain Drain
content: The Narendra Modi government is making a concentrated effort to bring back the brightest students and professionals overseas to India.

title: Delhi Man Poses As Top Government Official, Dupes People Of Rs 60 Lakh, Arrested
content: The police stated that the accused "posed as an Additional District Magistrate and preyed on trust," deceiving victims by projecting himself as a senior government officer with close links to top officials.

title: Deal Done, Trump's Envoy, Iran's Minister Head To Switzerland For Next Talks
content: JD Vance had canceled plans on Thursday to travel to Switzerland for the talks amid rising tension in Lebanon between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah.

title: 5 Killed As Israel Strikes Lebanon Despite Ceasefire With Hezbollah
content: Israel and Hezbollah agreed to the ceasefire on Friday following an escalation in hostilities in Lebanon, according to a US official.

title: Khan Sir Gets Interim Relief, No Arrest Till Next Hearing
content: The court upheld a 'no coercive action' order, directing the police not to take any harsh action against him until the next hearing in connection with the coaching institute firing case.

title: Trump Calls Netanyahu "Warrior PM" As US Intel Warns He May Ruin Iran Deal
content: Trump had earlier fumed at Benjamin Netanyahu over Israel's campaign against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon despite a deal with Tehran to end the war.

title: Rs 20 Note With Death Wish For Aunt Found In Andhra Temple Donation Box
content: The message, written in a requesting tone, read: "Swami, I can no longer bear the harassment from my aunt. Please make sure she dies soon."

title: "I Was Trying To Be Funny, Not The Boss": Trump On Viral G7 Remark
content: The US president drew laughs on Wednesday from his fellow G7 leaders for his quip as he walked into a meeting on the last day of their summit when he said, "I'm the boss."

title: 59 People Hospitalised In Maharashtra After Suspected Food Poisoning
content: According to the hospital official, the patients reported similar symptoms, including stomach pain, vomiting and diarrhoea, after consuming food from the same establishment.

title: "Her Journey Inspires People": PM Modi Greets President Murmu On Her Birthday
content: Born on June 20, 1958, in Uparbeda village of Odisha's Mayurbhanj district to a Santhal tribal family, President Murmu's life story is one of perseverance and determination.

title: "This Is Just A Trailer": Eknath Shinde Amid Team Thackeray Rebellion
content: This duelling comes against the backdrop of a fresh crisis for the Uddhav Thackeray-led Shiv Sena (UBT), with six of its nine Lok Sabha MPs expected to join the rival Shiv Sena led by Eknath Shinde.

title: Nobel Prize Winner John Jumper Quits Google DeepMind For Anthropic
content: John Jumper won the chemistry Nobel Prize alongside Deepmind CEO Demis Hassabis for creating an AI model that can predict the structure of proteins called AlphaFold.

title: "Be Quiet": Israeli Envoy, Top UN Official's Shouting Match At New York Meet
content: Israeli envoy Danny Danon clashed with UN official Vanessa Frazier over a report that blacklisted Israel for alleged sexual abuses in conflict.

title: Trump Asked Which World Leader He Admires Most, He Picks PM Modi, Xi Jinping
content: Donald Trump made the remarks on "The Axios Show" on Thursday, shortly after returning from a G7 summit in France that he described as "very dominant".

title: Trump-Meloni selfie row: Italy foreign minister cancels US visit
content: Italian Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani has cancelled a US visit following controversial remarks by Donald Trump about Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni. Trump claimed Meloni "begged" for a photo, a statement Meloni vehemently denied as "fabricated." The dispute has strained ties between the former allies, with Italian officials condemning Trump's "rants." The cancellation also postponed a key Italy-US business forum.

title: How snakebites push rural families deeper into poverty
content: The community-based retrospective study, conducted in Jagtial district of Telangana by researchers from the Hyderabad-based CSIR-Centre for Cellular and Molecular Biology (CCMB), highlights the deep and lasting impact of snakebites on agrarian households.

title: NTA to hold nationwide mock drill on June 20 ahead of NEET-UG re-exam
content: The re-examination will be conducted from 2 p.m. to 5:15 p.m. in pen-and-paper mode in 551 cities across India and 14 cities abroad for over 22.79 lakh candidates

title: World Refugee Day 2026: How refugees transformed Bengali food
content: From zero waste cooking and minimalist fish curries to Burmese noodle soups and Andaman settler kitchens, the flavours of displacement continue to shape what Bengal eats today.

title: Karnataka’s fight against suicide moves from hospitals to communities
content: As Karnataka grapples with one of the country’s highest suicide burdens, emerging evidence from NIMHANS-led initiatives suggests that prevention may lie not only in hospitals and psychiatric clinics, but also in schools, panchayats, primary health centres and everyday community networks. Afshan Yasmeen recounts how the initative, set to be scaled up across the State from July, works on the ground

title: Census 2027: The pressures of counting India
content: Enumerators working on the Census are facing several challenges in the field, including heat, connectivity issues, and safety concerns. Though the exercise is being conducted through a digital system designed for real-time monitoring, field workers say conditions on the ground and supervisory instructions are affecting data collection. Vijaita Singh reports on the challenges

title: Rebel Trinamool MPs must be disqualified, says Abhishek Banerjee
content: Abhishek Banerjee says the Constitution clearly mandates that at least two-thirds of the political party, and not just the legislative party, must agree to a merger for it to be considered valid

title: Heavy security in place as CJP stages second protest at Jantar Mantar
content: The Special Branch is keeping a close watch on developments at the protest site as well as related activity on social media, officials said

title: Help hospitals in Gaza and West Bank, Palestine envoy urges India
content: Hospitals running out of cancer drugs and other life-saving medicines, dialysis filters, insulin, surgical supplies, says Abu Shawesh

title: Emotional Uddhav offers to step down as Shiv Sena (UBT) president, Eknath Shinde hints at more defections
content: Mr. Thackeray calls for starting ‘Operation Lotus’ in response to ‘Operation Tiger’ allegedly carried out by the Shiv Sena to lure six of its nine Lok Sabha MPs into its fold and refuted allegations his party was likely to merge with the Congress

title: CWC inquiry finds more children ‘abused’ at worship centre in Kerala’s Pathanamthitta
content: Case was first registered against three staff members of the Christian worship centre for allegedly assaulting a boy

title: Lobbying for ministerial berths to regain momentum in Karnataka
content: 20 berths remain to be filled after 13 Ministers were sworn in along with Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar

title: Not blood, but ideology determines the heir, says Eknath Shinde
content: Targeting the UBT faction, Eknath Shinde said that those suggesting certain parties should merge with Congress should first introspect about their own declining strength.

title: Police, probe agencies get app to scan fingerprints on the go
content: Abhigyan app will let police check thumb impressions collected on portable scanners against NAFIS database within seconds; this will strengthen ground-level policing, says Home Minister Amit Shah

title: SIR explained: What Telangana voters need to know
content: As Telangana gears up for a door-to-door verification of voters, the Special Intensive Revision has emerged as one of the most closely watched electoral exercises in recent years. While election officials describe it as a routine effort to improve the accuracy of voter lists, political parties and electors have raised questions about implementation and the treatment of anomalies in voter data

title: Passenger’s leg gets stuck between metro train and platform; services disrupted briefly
content: The incident occurred between 10.20 a.m. and 10.30 a.m. at the Krantivira Sangolli Rayanna Railway Metro Station.
